differential_diagnoses: Spontaneous bacterial peritonitis (SBP) with associated septic shock
next_steps: Perform diagnostic paracentesis to analyze ascitic fluid for SBP (cell count, differential, and culture)
Obtain serial peripheral blood smears (thick and thin) and repeat rapid diagnostic testing for malaria
Order diagnostic testing for dengue (NS1 antigen and IgM/IgG serology) and leptospirosis (PCR and MAT)
Monitor cardiac troponin trends and echocardiography to assess progress of suspected myocarditis
Perform broad-spectrum testing for common tropical arboviruses (chikungunya, Zika) and bacterial pathogens (blood, urine, and sputum cultures)
Evaluate coagulation profile and fibrinogen levels to assess for disseminated intravascular coagulation
Ensure source control for potential bacterial translocation by continuing broad-spectrum antibiotic coverage
